free_text: Alpha induced reactions on natCd up to 38.5 MeV:
Experimental and theoretical studies of the
High purity (99.5%) natural Cd foils (15.5 um)
Two stacks, each containing 9 foils of natCd
interleaved with high-purity 12.30 um natCu monitor
Targets were irradiated in Faraday-cup type holder for
Several measurements from a couple of hours after EOB
till several days later were performed where the
distance source to detector was varied from 20 cm
The energy degradation was initially determined by
calculation using the formulas and tables of Andersen
and Ziegler and corrected on the basis of the
Quadrature sum of all individual contributions
number of target nuclei and non-uniformity (5%)
statistical error and peak area fit(1-8%)
Fitted excitation functions were used for
TS, Data section was corrected in subents
ts. Uncertainty of DATA point at 9.7 MeV
TS, Data section was corrected in subents
ts. Uncertainty of DATA point at 9.7 MeV
TS, Data in 002 and 003 were swapped.
VT. REACTION: SF4: M->M1+M2 and DECAY-DATA:
TS; CUM was removed from REACTION line
